topics.title: Gujarat Agriculture Overview
topics.content: Gujarat is one of India's leading agricultural states. Major crops include cotton (white gold), groundnut, wheat, and bajra. The state contributes significantly to India's cotton and oilseed production.
keyPoints: Gujarat accounts for ~30% of India's cotton production
Groundnut is the major kharif oilseed crop
Major rivers: Narmada, Sabarmati, Tapi irrigate farmlands
Sardar Sarovar Dam provides water to 18 lakh hectares
Bt Cotton was first commercially released in India in Gujarat (2002)

topics.title: Crop Seasons (Kharif, Rabi, Zaid)
topics.content: Indian agriculture follows three main crop seasons based on monsoon patterns.
keyPoints: Kharif (Jun-Nov): Cotton, Groundnut, Bajra, Jowar, Rice
Rabi (Nov-Apr): Wheat, Mustard, Gram, Barley
Zaid (Apr-Jun): Watermelon, Cucumber, Sunflower
Gujarat's main Kharif crops: Cotton, Groundnut, Maize
Gujarat's main Rabi crops: Wheat, Chickpea, Potato

topics.title: Soil Types in Gujarat
topics.content: Gujarat has diverse soil types suitable for various crops.
keyPoints: Black soil (Regur): Best for cotton, found in Saurashtra
Alluvial soil: Found near river banks, highly fertile
Sandy soil: Found in Kutch, suitable for drought-resistant crops
Red soil: Found in Chhota Udaipur area
Laterite soil: Found in hilly regions of Gujarat

topics.title: Agricultural Schemes & Programs
topics.content: Government schemes for farmers in Gujarat.
keyPoints: PM-KISAN: ₹6000/year direct income support to farmers
Mukhyamantri Kisan Sahay Yojana: Crop insurance scheme by Gujarat
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY): National crop insurance
Kisan Credit Card (KCC): Easy credit for farmers
Soil Health Card Scheme: Free soil testing for farmers

topics.title: Animal Husbandry & Dairy
topics.content: Gujarat is famous for its dairy cooperative movement.
keyPoints: AMUL (Anand Milk Union Ltd.) — world's largest dairy cooperative
Operation Flood: White Revolution led by Dr. Verghese Kurien
Major cattle breeds: Gir, Kankrej, Dangi
Gujarat ranks high in milk production in India
National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) headquartered in Anand, Gujarat

topics.title: Gujarat — Basic Facts
keyPoints: Official Name: Republic of India, State of Gujarat
Formation Date: 1 May 1960 (Bifurcation from Bombay State)
Capital: Gandhinagar | Largest City: Ahmedabad
Chief Minister: Bhupendrabhai Patel | Governor: Acharya Devvrat
Districts: 33 | Area: 1,96,024 sq km (7th largest state)
Population: ~6.04 crore (2011 census) | Literacy: 78.03%

topics.title: Historical Events & Heritage
topics.content: Key historical milestones related to Gujarat.
keyPoints: Harappan Civilization sites: Lothal, Dholavira (Kutch)
Somnath Temple: 12 Jyotirlingas, rebuilt multiple times
Champaner-Pavagadh: UNESCO World Heritage Site
Dandi March (1930): Gandhi's Salt March started from Sabarmati
Rani ki Vav (Patan): UNESCO World Heritage Site, built in 11th century
Modhera Sun Temple: Built by Bhimdev-I in 1026 AD

topics.title: Important Places & Tourism
topics.content: Major tourist and important places in Gujarat.
keyPoints: Gir Forest: Only wild Asiatic Lion habitat in world
Rann of Kutch: Largest salt desert in world
Statue of Unity: World's tallest statue (182m) — Sardar Patel
Sabarmati Ashram: Gandhi's residence in Ahmedabad
Marine National Park: First in India, located in Gulf of Kutch
Dwarka: One of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites

topics.title: Gujarat Government Structure
keyPoints: Legislature: Unicameral — Gujarat Vidhan Sabha (182 seats)
High Court: Gujarat High Court at Ahmedabad
Rajya Sabha seats: 11 | Lok Sabha seats: 26
Gram Panchayat: Smallest unit of rural local governance
Gram Sevak: Government employee serving at village level
GPSC: Gujarat Public Service Commission — conducts recruitment exams

topics.title: Economy & Industries
keyPoints: Gujarat contributes ~8% to India's GDP
Known as 'Manchester of India' for textile industry (Surat, Ahmedabad)
Largest producer of salt in India (Little Rann of Kutch)
Diamond cutting & polishing: Surat processes 80% of world's diamonds
GIFT City: India's first operational smart city and IFSC
Major ports: Kandla, Mundra, Pipavav — highest cargo traffic state

topics.title: Gujarati Vyakaran (Grammar) Basics
keyPoints: Gujarati is an Indo-Aryan language, derived from Sanskrit
Gujarati script has 13 vowels (સ્વર) and 36 consonants (વ્યંજન)
Nouns have 3 genders: masculine (પુ.), feminine (સ્ત્રी.), and neuter (નપ.)
Verb tenses: Present (વર્તમાન), Past (ભૂતકાળ), Future (ભવિષ્ય)
Sandhi: Joining of words/syllables — an important grammar concept

topics.title: Gujarati Literature & Poets
keyPoints: Narsinh Mehta: 'Aadikavi' of Gujarati literature — Vaishnav Jan To
Mirabai: Medieval poet-saint, devotee of Lord Krishna
Dayaram: Composed 'Garbi' poetry in Haveli Sangeet tradition
Jhaverchand Meghani: 'Rashtreeya Shayar' — national bard of Gujarat
Pannalal Patel: Ranjitram Award winner, wrote 'Manavini Bhavai'
Suresh Joshi: Founder of Gujarati modernism movement

topics.title: Muhavara & Kahevat (Idioms & Proverbs)
topics.content: Common Gujarati idioms and proverbs used in exams.
keyPoints: જ્યાં દૂઝ, ત્યાં ડૂઝ — Where there is milk, there are flies (prosperity attracts people)
ઊગ્યો સૂરજ ઊઘડ્યો — Rise with the sun (work hard from the start)
ગળ્યા ઘૂને ઝેર — Sweet words may hide poison
ઘર ઘર ની ગઝ — Every house has its own problems
Muhavara: Phrases with figurative meaning, differ from literal translation

topics.title: Nibandh (Essay Writing) Tips
topics.content: How to write good Gujarati essays for exams.
keyPoints: Introduction (પ્રસ્તાવના): Introduce the topic with a quote or fact
Body (વિષય-વિસ્તાર): Explain the topic in 3-4 paragraphs
Conclusion (ઉપસંહાર): Summarize and give your viewpoint
Use simple, clear Gujarati — avoid mixing too many English words
Common topics: Paryavaran, Swachh Bharat, Atmanirbhar Bharat, Digital India

topics.title: Parts of Speech
topics.content: The 8 main parts of speech in English grammar.
keyPoints: Noun: Name of a person, place, thing (e.g., Gujarat, farmer, water)
Pronoun: Replaces a noun (e.g., he, she, it, they)
Verb: Action or state (e.g., run, is, write)
Adjective: Describes a noun (e.g., green, large, beautiful)
Adverb: Modifies verb/adjective (e.g., quickly, very, well)
Preposition: Shows relationship (e.g., in, on, at, with)
Conjunction: Joins words/clauses (e.g., and, but, or, because)
Interjection: Exclamations (e.g., Oh!, Wow!, Alas!)

topics.title: Tenses — Quick Reference
keyPoints: Simple Present: I write / He writes (habit/fact)
Present Continuous: I am writing (action happening now)
Simple Past: I wrote (completed action)
Past Continuous: I was writing (ongoing past action)
Simple Future: I will write (future action)
Present Perfect: I have written (recently completed)
Past Perfect: I had written (completed before another past action)

topics.title: Synonyms & Antonyms (Common Words)
keyPoints: Brave → Synonym: Courageous | Antonym: Cowardly
Ancient → Synonym: Old | Antonym: Modern/New
Abundant → Synonym: Plentiful | Antonym: Scarce
Benevolent → Synonym: Kind | Antonym: Cruel
Diligent → Synonym: Hardworking | Antonym: Lazy
Transparent → Synonym: Clear | Antonym: Opaque

topics.title: One Word Substitutions
topics.content: Single words that replace descriptive phrases — common in exams.
keyPoints: Omnivore: One who eats both plants and animals
Bibliophile: One who loves books
Philatelist: One who collects stamps
Omniscient: One who knows everything
Ambidextrous: One who can use both hands equally
Insolvent: One who cannot pay debts (bankrupt)
Posthumous: Occurring after death
